Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Deconstruct and Understand the Models: Goal: Parse the Python code to understand the structure of model1 and model2. POWL Model 1 Analysis: Nodes: Post, Screen, Decide, Interview, Onboard, Payroll, Close. Edges: Post -> Screen, Screen -> Decide, Screen -> Interview, Decide -> Onboard, Onboard -> Payroll, Payroll -> Close. Structure Visualization: Post starts. Post leads to Screen. Screen splits into Decide and Interview. Decide leads to Onboard. Onboard leads to Payroll. Payroll leads to Close. Implicit Logic: Screen happens. Then Decide and Interview can happen in parallel. Critical Path Check: Post -> Screen -> Decide -> Onboard -> Payroll -> Close. Anomaly Detection: Decide and Interview are concurrent. You could make a decision without interviewing.

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: POWL Model 2 Analysis: Nodes: Post, Screen, Interview, Decide, loop_onboarding, xor_payroll, Close. loop_onboarding = *(Onboard, skip). This means Onboard happens at least once, can happen multiple times. xor_payroll = XOR(Payroll, skip). Payroll is optional. Edges: Post -> Screen, Post -> Interview, Interview -> Decide, Decide -> loop_onboarding, loop_onboarding -> xor_payroll, xor_payroll -> Close. Structure Visualization: Post splits to Screen and Interview. Interview leads to Decide. Critical Path Check: Post -> Interview -> Decide -> Onboard -> Payroll -> Close. Anomaly Detection: Screen and Interview are concurrent. Payroll is optional.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Compare Anomalies and Severity: Model 1 Anomalies: Decide and Interview are concurrent. Impact: Decision might be made without interview data. Severity: High. Model 2 Anomalies: Screen and Interview are concurrent. Impact: Interviewing without screening risks wasting resources. Severity: Medium. Payroll is optional. Impact: Employee might not be paid. Severity: Critical.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Evaluate Normative Alignment: Standard Hire-to-Retire: Post -> Screen -> Interview -> Decide -> Onboard -> Payroll -> Close. Model 1: Post -> Screen -> (Interview || Decide) -> Onboard -> Payroll -> Close. Allows hiring without interview. Model 2: Post -> (Screen || Interview) -> Decide -> Onboard -> Payroll? -> Close. Allows interviewing without screening. Allows hiring without payroll. Comparison: Model 1 fails on Interview before Decide. Model 2 fails on Screen before Interview AND Payroll mandatory. Model 2 has more anomalies.

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Re-check Model 2 Ancestors: Edges: Post->Screen, Post->Interview, Interview->Decide. Path to Decide: Post->Interview->Decide. Screen is not on this path. Decide can occur without Screen.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Conclusion: Model 1: Screen is mandatory before Decide. Interview is optional relative to Decide. Payroll is mandatory. Model 2: Screen is not mandatory before Decide. Payroll is optional. Model 1 is closer to normative.

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Review and Refine: Critique: Interpretations of loop_onboarding and xor_payroll correct. Model 1 has Screen -> Interview and Screen -> Decide. Model 2 has concurrent Screen and Interview, Decide without Screen. Result stands: Model 2 is worse.
